title: Reading PC Convergence: When Are Beliefs Stable?

runtime.expected_outcome: F total drops monotonically. When Delta-F < tol, beliefs are stable. Diverging errors indicate precision imbalance.

audiences.kid: When the score stops changing, you're done thinking. If the score bounces around, something's off.
audiences.real: Monitor F across PredictiveCodingPass iterations. Convergence means Delta-F under tol; oscillation means precision/learning-rate is too high.
audiences.equation: Delta-F = F^(k) - F^(k-1). Converged when |Delta-F| < tol. Divergence when |Delta-F| grows -> lower eta or lower pi.
audiences.derivation: Gradient descent on F under Lipschitz gradient converges for learning rate eta <= 1/L. Higher pi grows L, so eta must shrink proportionally.

orchestrate.objective: Add a simple convergence diagnostic on top of the predictive-coding sweep.
orchestrate.context: Run sweep with varying eta; plot F(k) and |Delta-F(k)|.

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), Ch 5.
Bogacz (2017), A tutorial on the free-energy framework for modelling perception and learning.
